keyLegislation: Australian Charities and Not-for-profits Commission Act 2012 (ACNC Act)
Corporations Act 2001 (for incorporated associations and companies limited by guarantee)
Trustee Act (State/Territory specific, for charitable trusts)
Anti-Money Laundering and Counter-Terrorism Financing Act 2006 (AML/CTF Act)
keyConsiderations: NFPs must act within their stated charitable or not-for-profit purposes — Bitcoin investment must be consistent with the organisation's constitution and objects
Board or committee of management has fiduciary duties to act in the best interests of the organisation and apply funds towards its purposes
Charities registered with the ACNC must comply with governance standards, including the duty of care and duty to act in furtherance of the charity's purposes
Tax-exempt status (income tax exemption under Division 50 ITAA 1997) requires that the organisation's income and assets are applied solely towards its exempt purposes
Deductible Gift Recipient (DGR) status imposes additional restrictions — DGR funds must be used for the specific DGR purpose
Investment of reserves is generally permitted if it constitutes prudent management of the organisation's assets, but speculative investments may be challenged
State and territory fundraising legislation may impose restrictions on how donated funds can be invested
Donor expectations and grant conditions may restrict how funds are invested — check all grant agreements and donation conditions
ACNC Annual Information Statement requires disclosure of financial information, which would include Bitcoin holdings
Charity governance standards require that the responsible persons (board/committee members) exercise reasonable care and diligence in investment decisions
Public trust and reputational risk are heightened for NFPs — Bitcoin holdings may attract scrutiny from donors, members, and the public
NFPs with income tax exemption status do not pay CGT on Bitcoin gains, but this exemption is conditional on continuing to meet the requirements for exemption
commonRisks: Breach of fiduciary duty if the board does not exercise adequate care and diligence in the investment decision
Loss of tax-exempt status if Bitcoin investment is deemed inconsistent with the organisation's exempt purposes
Reputational damage if donors, members, or the public view the Bitcoin investment as irresponsible or speculative
Breach of grant conditions if grant funds are invested in Bitcoin without the grantor's approval
Breach of DGR requirements if DGR-held funds are invested in assets not consistent with the DGR purpose
Volatility risk leading to a material reduction in funds available for charitable or NFP purposes
Inadequate governance and documentation leading to personal liability for board members
Breach of state/territory fundraising legislation regarding the investment of donated funds
Loss of Bitcoin due to security failures, with no insurance or recourse mechanisms in place
ACNC compliance action for failure to meet governance standards or financial reporting requirements
Conflict of interest if a board member has personal financial interests in cryptocurrency
recommendedProfessionals: Registered Tax Agent with NFP and cryptocurrency experience
Auditor (if the organisation is required to be audited)
